excerpt: A short policy brief format for dialogue support and informed policymaking.
authorRole: Policy dialogue, editorial production, and publication development

Policy Brief Format

This brief highlights current regional security and diplomacy priorities across the Mediterranean space.

  • Policy-relevant framing
  • Dialogue support orientation
  • National and transnational perspective

Key Notes

  2. Strengthen collective reflection.

title: Red Sea Corridors: Trade, Security, and Regional Power
excerpt: A short audio brief unpacking the strategic significance of the Red Sea and the regional actors shaping its future.
authorRole: Podcast and editorial brief production on regional security and maritime strategy
contentHtml:
Red Sea Corridors

This audio release introduces the strategic significance of the Red Sea as a corridor shaped by trade, maritime risk, diplomacy, and regional competition.

What This Release Covers

  • Regional security implications of maritime disruptions
  • Trade and logistics pressure across connected routes
  • The role of middle powers in shaping corridor governance
  • Why Red Sea developments matter for Mediterranean policy debates

Editorial Note

This release is presented as a short-form audio brief linked to the Mediterranean Platform’s wider work on regional cooperation, security, and policy dialogue.

title: Mediterranean Talks: In Conversation
excerpt: An editorial update on talks, events, and policy dialogue priorities.
contentHtml:
Mediterranean Talks

This editorial stream shares updates on events, publications, and policy dialogue across the broader Mediterranean.

Current Focus

Work concentrates on governance transitions, regional security, diplomacy, and scenario-making for informed policymaking.

title: The Red Sea: Divided by Water, United by Opportunities
excerpt: A multi-disciplinary publication on regional opportunity structures, cooperation pathways, and strategic dialogue.
authorRole: Edited volume on regional strategy, maritime security, and policy dialogue
contentHtml:
The recent maritime crisis in the Red Sea has brought this vital global waterway back to the fore. The region’s destiny is no longer being decided only by distant powers, but by assertive regional actors, shifting alliances, and new strategic calculations.

This publication provides a cross-regional reading of the Red Sea and the Horn of Africa, connecting Middle Eastern and African perspectives in one analytical frame.

It offers a policy-relevant basis for dialogue, scenario-building, and long-term strategic reflection.

title: Distribution of Powers in Libya's Future Local Governance System
excerpt: Policy paper on governance architecture and local institutional design for stability and inclusive policymaking.
authorRole: Policy analysis on governance reform and institutional design
contentHtml:
This paper examines the allocation of powers in Libya’s future local governance architecture and its implications for institutional balance, accountability, and implementation.

It focuses on realistic pathways for reform that can support both local legitimacy and national coherence.

title: Subnational Governance in Divided Societies: Learning from Yemen to Inform Libya's Peace Process
excerpt: Comparative insights connecting local governance, peace process design, and practical dialogue support.
authorRole: Comparative governance and peace process research
contentHtml:
Drawing lessons from Yemen, this paper connects subnational governance design with peace process architecture in Libya.

It highlights how local governance can be integrated into wider political settlements and transitional dialogue formats.

title: Youth as Catalysts for Shaping Libya's Future Pathways for Inclusion in National Dialogue and Vision-Making
excerpt: Evidence-led paper on youth inclusion, national dialogue, and long-term vision-making processes.
authorRole: Research on inclusion, youth participation, and dialogue design
contentHtml:
This paper looks at how young people can be meaningfully included in Libya’s national dialogue and longer-term vision-making processes.

It connects policy design with participatory practice, mentorship, and institution-building.

title: Africa Nexus: Building an African-European Knowledge Ecosystem
excerpt: A platform briefing connecting experts, young scholars, and policymakers through policy analyses and knowledge exchange.
contentHtml:
Africa Nexus is designed as a policy-facing crossroads for African and European experts, researchers, and institutions.

Its focus is on building a durable knowledge ecosystem that can support sustained dialogue, publications, and practical cooperation.

title: Hydrogen Valleys and Sustainable Development in Algeria: Pivoting from Hydrocarbons to an Inclusive Euro-Mediterranean Hydrogen Economy
excerpt: Research on energy transition, industrial strategy, and climate-linked development in North Africa.
authorRole: Energy transition and industrial strategy analysis
contentHtml:
This report examines hydrogen valleys, industrial policy, and just transition questions in Algeria.

It situates national planning within a wider Euro-Mediterranean energy economy and long-term development framework.

title: Gulf Platform: Europe-Gulf Dialogue on Geopolitical, Economic, and Societal Trends
excerpt: Policy-relevant research and dialogue support for decision-makers working across regional security and long-term cooperation files.
authorRole: Platform briefing on Europe-Gulf dialogue and strategic trends
contentHtml:
This publication outlines the Gulf Platform’s research and dialogue agenda across geopolitical, economic, and societal files.

It frames Europe-Gulf relations as a long-term policy conversation rather than a narrow crisis response track.
